body.u: Looking back onto my life, I feel that I filled my time wisely. I like to think of the hapierhappier times I had of the joy’s and pleasure of my younger days. I started my older days in 1976 when I left school, I then went on to do three years at college, after my school type days were over I was fit for a career for what I had trained for.
I went off to work in a large hotel in London looking after foods and wines, this was what I had dreamed of and there I was doing my job.
Life seems to come in stages, when I look over the past I feel I’ve now finally reached the top.
I carried on work for many years, earned enough to keep us going, as now I was married, we worked hard to collect for a fine house and a few luxurysluxuries that life needed.

After four years of marriage the first of the family arrived, I left my job and started to play the part of housewife. Life seemed to be hard, and being a mother it was even harder. After the second child was born it seemed to be a little more like home. As time passed rapidly the family grew up the first boy had just left school and was now out to work.

As my family were now a little older, I had decided to work again, I started my own little business at home. I was making lovely cake for weddings birthdays etc. The family was young no longer both children now at work it was sad to watch I was young once dreaming of the years when I could go to work, have a home and a family and here I was age going up getting on.

Not a lot happened to me in my fourty’sfourties but I could be happy and watch my family growing. The time I started to feel older was when I was told the fine news that I was to be a grandmother. It was lovely but I felt I was getting on. As my family had grown so totoo did the grandchildren, as life has it good times there totoo was sadness, my husband is now passed on and I can just sit back and recall all of the happy memorysmemories of my life.
